title: Astephanus luteus
taxonomy: Astephanus luteus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae, commonly known as the broomrape family. This family is a group of flowering plants that are primarily parasitic, obtaining their nutrients from other plants. Astephanus luteus is native to various regions, including parts of Africa and Asia. It is a plant that has adapted to thrive in areas with limited soil resources by forming a symbiotic relationship with its host plant. The classification of Astephanus luteus reflects its unique characteristics as a parasitic flowering plant.
